Note the epsilon in style of four-bar sigma with extended middle bar
Orsi, 1876-, Notizie degli scavi di antichità
C2 BCE to C1 CE (Ferrua suggested C2 CE)
Text encoding: Marta Capano; Andoni Llamazares, edition source:
Hellenistic (assigned to C2 CE by Ferrua, without reference to the archaeological context of re-use)
The text is laid out over seven lines, somehwat unevenly spaced and sized, largest in lines 3-5, smallest in the final line, with some use of interpuncts.
Letters tending in some cases towards cursive (e.g. M), with I and T indistinguishable, E and F tall and narrow. Wedge shaped serifs at stroke ends.
Agnello, 1953, Silloge di iscrizioni paleocristiane della Sicilia
Ferrua, 1989, Note e giunte alle iscrizioni cristiane antiche della Sicilia
Manganaro, 1993, Greco nei pagi e latino nelle città della Sicilia romana tra I e VI sec. d.C.
3rd century CE or later, cursive lettering
The names are split into two columns, with the concluding salutation following below these; there is a mark of separation between the two names to clarify this.
Bernabò Brea, 1876-, Notizie degli scavi di antichità
Ferrua, 1982-1983, Le iscrizioni datate della Sicilia paleocristiana
Regular left margin, one world per line.
private collection (villa of the contessa Pallavicini, Panarea)
Giustolisi, 1995, Vulcano. Introduzione alla storia e all'archeologia dell'antica Hiera
private collection (villa of the contessa pallavicini, panarea)
Text irregularly fills the upper part of the stele, words broken across line ends
Large crudely cut letters, lunate sigma, straigh-bar alpha
note small open omega, broken bar alpha, standard sigma
Part of a structure 'di eta ellenistica', perhaps 3rd century BCE.
Bernabò Brea and Cavalier, 1994, Meligunis Lipara VII. Lipari. Contrada Diana. Scavo XXXVI in proprieta Zagami (1975-1984)
3rd-2nd century BCE (editors suggest C3 due to quality of lettering)
Bernabò Brea, 1982, Iscrizioni funerarie di cittadini eleati a Lipari
Note pi with short right hasta
The inscription is between two irregular engraved guide lines
The inscription is between two thin engraved guide lines
Line 1, rho 65mm, line 2, chi 69mm, rho 65mm. Sign on the reverse 29.5cm
Line 1: omicron 45mm, Line 2: omicron 40mm, Line 3: omicron 48mm

Two lines of deeply cut, somewhat irregular letters, on the upper part of the front face of the stele, with the second line somewhat compressed at the end.
Somewhat irregular plain letters, with the T of line 2 extended (120mm), the penultimate I in ligature, and the final S small to fit in.
In the cloister of the Basilica Concattedrale di San Bartolomeo, Lipari
Text is roughly centered on the upper half of the stele, with an area of erasure below. The lines are somewhat irregular in their layout.
Ferrua, 1941, Epigrafica sicula pagana e cristiana
Epitaph erected by Hilara for her daughter
Text over two lines, large somewhat irregular letters across the middle of the front face.
epitaph erected by hilara for her daughter
Epitaph of Aulus Hirtius Iunianus and Tucuria Alcimilla
The text is approximately centred over 5 lines, with an incised horizontal margin below the inscription.
Currently in the Norman cloister of the castle
epitaph of aulus hirtius iunianus and tucuria alcimilla
The text is centered on the stone, filling the available field, with large if slightly irregular letters and spacing.
Letters are tall, with straight horizontal serifs, and curved strokes to the X; I is extended and final O on line 1 is small.
